summaries.grain: One row per public GitHub event within the date encoded by the physical table name.
repeated_column_rules: No numbered repeated columns; repetition is across physical daily tables named _YYYYMMDD.
quote_spelling_rules: Daily table names start with an underscore and must be quoted when needed.
Use DAY._YYYYMMDD spelling for physical daily partitions.

summaries.purpose: Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages metrics by area and industry.
summaries.grain: One row per area/geoid per quarterly physical table, with industries stored as repeated metric-column families.
repeated_column_rules: month3_emplvl_<industry_code>_<industry_name> stores employment level metrics.
avg_wkly_wage_<industry_code>_<industry_name> stores average weekly wage metrics.
qtrly_estabs_<industry_code>_<industry_name> stores establishment counts.
lq_<metric>_<industry_code>_<industry_name> stores location quotients for the same metric families.
quote_spelling_rules: Quarter table names start with an underscore and must be quoted when needed.
Keep industry code tokens such as 44_45 and 31_33 exactly as spelled.

summaries.purpose: American Community Survey estimates split by geography, year, and estimate window.
summaries.grain: One row per Census geography key in the physical table's geography/year/window.
repeated_column_rules: Most non-geo_id columns are ACS estimate measures, including population, housing, income, education, commuting, employment, race, and age fields.
The table name encodes geography such as COUNTY, PLACE, CBSA, BLOCKGROUP, CENSUSTRACT, or CONGRESSIONALDISTRICT.
The final suffix encodes survey year plus 1YR, 3YR, or 5YR estimate window.
inclusive_ranges: Observed tables use years from 2007 through 2021 depending on geography and estimate window.
quote_spelling_rules: Use geo_id exactly; do not rewrite it as geoid.
Keep compact table geography spellings such as CENSUSTRACT and BLOCKGROUP.

summaries.purpose: US county and state COVID-19 case/death counts from USAFacts.
summaries.grain: CONFIRMED_CASES and DEATHS are wide county rows with one date column per day; SUMMARY is long-form county-date rows.
repeated_column_rules: CONFIRMED_CASES and DEATHS repeat daily count columns named _YYYY_MM_DD.
SUMMARY uses date, deaths, and confirmed_cases as regular columns instead of one column per day.
inclusive_ranges: Wide date columns span 2020 through 2023 in _YYYY_MM_DD spelling.
quote_spelling_rules: Wide date columns begin with an underscore and must be quoted when referenced.
Use county_fips_code and state_fips_code exactly as spelled.

summaries.purpose: Johns Hopkins CSSE COVID-19 time-series counts and summary rows.
summaries.grain: Wide count tables are one row per province/country location with one date column per day; SUMMARY is one row per location-date.
repeated_column_rules: CONFIRMED_CASES, DEATHS, and RECOVERED_CASES repeat daily columns named _M_D_YY.
SUMMARY uses date, confirmed, deaths, recovered, and active as regular columns.
inclusive_ranges: Wide date columns use month_day_two_digit_year suffixes from 2020 through 2023.
quote_spelling_rules: Wide date columns begin with an underscore and must be quoted when referenced.
Wide tables use country_or_region, while SUMMARY uses country_region.

summaries.purpose: Google COVID-19 Open Data public-health, mobility, policy, demographic, and search trend facts.
summaries.grain: One row per location_key and date, with location hierarchy columns and many thematic metric families.
repeated_column_rules: new_* and cumulative_* columns store case, death, testing, hospitalization, intensive care, recovery, and vaccination metrics.
search_trends_* columns store symptom/search-interest measures.
mobility_* and policy columns store behavioral and public-health intervention measures.
age_bin_* and population_age_* columns store age-bucket demographics.
inclusive_ranges: The date column stores the observation date; physical table names are not date partitions.
quote_spelling_rules: Use location_key exactly for geography joins.
Keep subregion1 and subregion2 spellings with numeric suffixes.

summaries.purpose: Imaging Data Commons DICOM instance, series, study, and curated metadata.
summaries.grain: Rows represent DICOM entities or curated metadata views, usually keyed by SOPInstanceUID, SeriesInstanceUID, StudyInstanceUID, or collection fields.
repeated_column_rules: Tag_* columns store DICOM tag-derived fields.
collection_*, series_*, study_*, and instance_* columns store entity-level IDC metadata.
gcs_* and aws_* columns store cloud object location metadata.
inclusive_ranges: IDC_V17 tables are fixed physical tables, not date partitions.
quote_spelling_rules: Preserve DICOM camel-case spellings such as SOPInstanceUID and StudyInstanceUID.
Preserve IDC lowercase prefixes such as collection_, series_, study_, and instance_.

summaries.purpose: TCGA PanCancer clinical, biospecimen, and annotation metadata.
summaries.grain: Rows describe cases, samples, clinical attributes, biospecimens, or annotations depending on the table.
repeated_column_rules: Clinical tables contain many disease, staging, treatment, demographic, and outcome columns rather than numbered repeated fields.
Versioned clinical table names such as CLINICAL_V1 and CLINICAL_V1_1 should be treated as separate physical tables.
inclusive_ranges: Clinical version suffixes are explicit table names, not generated ranges.
quote_spelling_rules: Use case_barcode exactly for case-level joins.
Preserve CLINICAL_V1_1 spelling with both underscores.

summaries.purpose: National provider identifier registry and healthcare taxonomy reference data.
summaries.grain: NPI tables are one row per provider record; taxonomy tables are one row per provider taxonomy code entry.
repeated_column_rules: healthcare_provider_taxonomy_code_N and healthcare_provider_primary_taxonomy_switch_N repeat taxonomy assignments.
provider_license_number_N and provider_license_number_state_code_N repeat license fields.
other_provider_identifier_N, other_provider_identifier_state_N, other_provider_identifier_issuer_N, and other_provider_identifier_type_code_N repeat other identifier fields.
inclusive_ranges: Numbered NPI_RAW field families observed through double-digit suffixes.
quote_spelling_rules: Use npi exactly when present; do not expand it to provider id.
Keep long provider_* field names exactly as spelled.

summaries.purpose: US Forest Service Forest Inventory and Analysis plot, tree, condition, and population estimation data.
summaries.grain: Rows represent forest plots, trees, plot-tree links, conditions, or population estimation units depending on the table.
repeated_column_rules: plot_* columns describe plot-level identifiers, geography, survey, and status.
tree_* columns describe tree identifiers, inventory cycles, species/class/status, and measurements.
population_* columns describe evaluation, estimation unit, stratum, and assignment metadata.
inclusive_ranges: USFS_FIA uses fixed physical tables, not generated date partitions.
quote_spelling_rules: Preserve plot_ and tree_ prefixes.
Use sequence-number columns exactly as spelled.

summaries.purpose: COVID-19 symptom search trend scores by geography and daily or weekly cadence.
summaries.grain: One row per date and geography level, with many symptom_* search-interest metrics.
repeated_column_rules: symptom_* columns store search-interest scores for individual symptoms or conditions.
The table name encodes geography level and DAILY or WEEKLY cadence.
inclusive_ranges: Cadence is encoded by the physical table suffix DAILY or WEEKLY.
quote_spelling_rules: Preserve symptom_* metric spellings.
Use SUB_REGION_1 and SUB_REGION_2 exactly in table names.
